game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **던전 워페어 (Dungeon Warfare)**: Players defend their dungeon by strategically placing 26 unique trap types, each with 3 upgrade tiers, along predetermined paths to eliminate waves of invading adventurers. To place a trap, players tap on an empty grid space, select a trap from the build menu, and tap again to confirm placement, using earned gold to purchase and upgrade traps during a mission. Traps range from direct damage (e.g., Dart Traps) to crowd control (e.g., Spring Traps) and minion summoning (e.g., Summon Portals), interacting with environmental elements like rolling boulders or lava flows for additional damage or path manipulation. Progression involves conquering over 40 distinct dungeon maps, earning experience points from defeated enemies to permanently enhance trap stats between missions, and customizing difficulty by combining up to 12 "difficulty runes." The objective is to prevent a set number of adventurers (e.g., 20) from reaching the dungeon core, with failure occurring if too many escape.
game_info.description: Defend your dungeon core from waves of invading adventurers by strategically placing traps along their path. Purchase and upgrade traps using gold earned from defeating enemies. Each trap has unique abilities - from dart shooters to spring launchers to lava flows. Prevent 20 adventurers from reaching your core to win. Watch your gold and plan carefully, as trap placement is permanent once confirmed!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate trap selection menu and grid positions
Space: Confirm trap placement or upgrade selection
Z: Cancel current action or deselect trap
Shift: Speed up enemy wave spawning (hold)
Enter: Start game from START screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game (returns to START screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic trap placement mechanics and gold management. Validates that traps can be placed on valid grid positions and that gold is correctly deducted. Ensures enemies spawn and move along the path.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Place a few dart traps near the start of the path to damage early enemies. Monitor gold accumulation and enemy health reduction. Test cancellation and menu navigation.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Traps are successfully placed, enemies take damage when passing through trap zones, gold is earned from kills, and the game continues without errors for several waves.
TEST_1.description: Tests basic trap placement mechanics and gold management. Validates that traps can be placed on valid grid positions and that gold is correctly deducted. Ensures enemies spawn and move along the path.
TEST_1.strategy: Place a few dart traps near the start of the path to damage early enemies. Monitor gold accumulation and enemy health reduction. Test cancellation and menu navigation.
TEST_1.expected: Traps are successfully placed, enemies take damage when passing through trap zones, gold is earned from kills, and the game continues without errors for several waves.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests optimal strategy to achieve victory condition. Validates that strategic trap placement and upgrades can prevent 20 enemies from reaching the core, resulting in GAME_OVER_WIN.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Place high-damage traps at strategic choke points early in the path. Upgrade key traps to maximize damage output. Focus on dart traps and spring traps for knockback. Use lava flows to create damage zones.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: All 20 enemy waves are defeated before reaching the core. The game transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN state, displaying victory message and final score.
TEST_2.description: Tests optimal strategy to achieve victory condition. Validates that strategic trap placement and upgrades can prevent 20 enemies from reaching the core, resulting in GAME_OVER_WIN.

TEST_2.expected: All 20 enemy waves are defeated before reaching the core. The game transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN state, displaying victory message and final score.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests player loss condition. Validates that allowing too many enemies to reach the core triggers GAME_OVER_LOSE state correctly.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Place no traps or minimal ineffective traps. Allow enemies to reach the core unimpeded. Verify that the escaped enemy counter increments correctly.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: When 20 enemies escape to the core, the game transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE state with appropriate game over message and final statistics.
TEST_3.description: Tests player loss condition. Validates that allowing too many enemies to reach the core triggers GAME_OVER_LOSE state correctly.
TEST_3.strategy: Place no traps or minimal ineffective traps. Allow enemies to reach the core unimpeded. Verify that the escaped enemy counter increments correctly.
TEST_3.expected: When 20 enemies escape to the core, the game transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE state with appropriate game over message and final statistics.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests trap upgrade mechanics. Validates that traps can be upgraded through multiple tiers and that upgrades improve trap effectiveness.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Place a dart trap, earn gold, then upgrade it to tier 2 and tier 3. Observe damage increase and visual changes. Compare effectiveness against enemies.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Traps successfully upgrade, gold is deducted correctly, visual indicators show upgrade level, and higher-tier traps deal more damage to enemies.
TEST_4.description: Tests trap upgrade mechanics. Validates that traps can be upgraded through multiple tiers and that upgrades improve trap effectiveness.
TEST_4.strategy: Place a dart trap, earn gold, then upgrade it to tier 2 and tier 3. Observe damage increase and visual changes. Compare effectiveness against enemies.
TEST_4.expected: Traps successfully upgrade, gold is deducted correctly, visual indicators show upgrade level, and higher-tier traps deal more damage to enemies.

TEST_5.test_description: Tests multiple trap type interactions. Validates that different trap types work together and provide varied strategic options.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Place a mix of dart traps (damage), spring traps (knockback), and lava flows (area damage). Observe how enemies interact with multiple trap types in sequence.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Each trap type functions correctly with its unique behavior. Spring traps push enemies back, lava creates persistent damage zones, and dart traps fire projectiles. Combined effects slow enemy progress effectively.
TEST_5.description: Tests multiple trap type interactions. Validates that different trap types work together and provide varied strategic options.
